data.title: [end-session]: manual merge fallback leaves worktree not auto-removable by claude-safe
data.description:
## Problem

When a host project has no `merge-to-main.sh` script, `/dso:end-session` falls back to a manual merge. In the observed case, the merge was performed via `git push origin worktree-branch:main` (a direct push), which pushes commits to the remote but does NOT update the local `main` ref.

After this:
- `git merge-base --is-ancestor "$BRANCH" origin/main` → MERGED ✓ (checked origin)
- `git merge-base --is-ancestor "$BRANCH" main` → NOT MERGED ✗ (local main not updated)

`claude-safe`'s `_offer_worktree_cleanup` uses the local check (`main`, not `origin/main`), so the worktree fails the `is_merged` condition and is not auto-removed.

## Root Cause

The end-session fallback to manual merge used `git push origin branch:main` instead of a proper `git checkout main && git merge --no-ff branch && git push`. The pre-bash hook blocked the `cd` to the main repo directory (CLAUDE.md rule 11 — never cd into main repo from a worktree session), so the local merge was impossible.

Two problems compound:
1. The `is_merged` check in end-session Step 4.75 used `origin/main` in the fallback verification but claude-safe checks local `main`
2. The pre-bash hook correctly blocked the cd, but end-session had no compliant path to do a local merge from within the worktree

## Observed Log

```
No merge script available — doing a manual merge.
fatal: 'main' is already used by worktree at '/Users/joeoakhart/parity-scan'
BLOCKED: Bash command cd's into the main repo from a worktree session.
git push origin worktree-20260409-182811:main  # workaround
git merge-base --is-ancestor "$BRANCH" origin/main → MERGED
```

## Expected Behavior

After end-session completes, `git merge-base --is-ancestor "$BRANCH" main` (local) returns 0 so that claude-safe can auto-remove the worktree.

## Fix Directions

Option A: After a manual push-to-main, end-session should run `git fetch origin main:main` to update the local ref, ensuring local `main` reflects the push.

Option B: end-session's Step 4.75 should verify against both local and remote: if local check fails but `origin/main` check passes, run `git fetch origin main:main` before re-checking.

Option C: The end-session skill should detect the no-merge-script case earlier and guide the user to run from the main repo checkout instead of the worktree.
